reviews.review_msg: He has as a very patronizing way of speaking to students - especially if you get a question wrong or don't know the answer. Likes to call on people in class when no one answers his questions.
He doesn't convey material very effectively. Some of the readings are interesting his teaching style isn't amazing but not definitively the worst at SFU just not good.
Pretty weird guy. Rambles about irrelevant things and doesn't always clearly connect it back to the course. Would definitely recommend taking this course with someone else if you can
Steve is just a very strange guy. The class wasn't overly hard and you did learn lots.

Steve was a great professor who was very entertaining and really cared for his students. He answers email even on weekends and truly attends to his students. Lecture was heavily based on what he says in class rather than slides and textbooks but he makes lectures entertaining and enjoying. Makes great humour in class.

You know, I bet you're scrolling through these reviews, thinking "god I should avoid Steve." However, if you have a sense of humor, and enjoy interesting people, then I would definitely recommend Steve. It's an experience you can't get with any other professor: he tells jokes all class and truly knows his material. Also you don't need the textbook.

Steve is entertaining but rude. Tough grader, the assignments don't have a grading rubric which makes it extra confusing. The book and slides don't reflect what he's teaching in class, but if you pay attention, the assignments are not that hard.

This guy is insufferable. He rambles about nonsense unrelated to class material and won't even get past the title lecture slide until an hour into the lecture. He talks about controversial topics that make everyone uncomfortable and is condescending when you don't share the same viewpoints as him.

Steve certaintly shocked everyone in class every lecture by the things he would say. Although he discusses controversial topics all the time, it somehow makes the class go by fast. His grading criteria is very vague, so I would def ask him a lot of questions.

He loves talking about unrelated stuff. I still don't get how some topic is related to marketing and why Steve keeps bringing it up. The ads he shows in class are kind of weird too. The two group assignments were unclear in terms of grading, and the instructions were also unclear. If you can, just avoid this him.

Gets really wrapped up in his own politics. Spent like 30 minutes arguing that the statement "From the river to the sea" was antisemitic, the dude is experiencing crazyyyyy cognitive dissonance. If you wanna take a lecture from a zionist go ahead.

If you want to feel uncomfortable for 3 hours then this is the class. He loves to revolve discussions around controversial topics and will call on people if no one answers. If you answer, he'll make you feel bad about it if it's wrong. Some topics in the class are actually interesting and group papers are worth a lot and super unclear.
Group assignments were very confusing, counted for a large portion of your grade. Hard marker.
Steve is no easygoing professor. Get ready to read many articles and be prepared for group assignments that are not easy. He is a tough grader on the assignments, the final is worth 40%.
